title: Default and Direct Exchanges, Work Queues, and Fair Dispatch · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers default and direct exchanges, work queues, and fair dispatch into a new direct-routing work distribution service. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of direct-routing work distribution service with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.
Design direct exchange bindings that keep producer intent stable while consumer queues evolve.
Use one queue with multiple consumers for load distribution and state that each delivery goes to one active consumer at a time.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Default Exchange Coupling
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 9690 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then exhaust the retry age before the attempt count reaches its maximum. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Default Exchange Coupling.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Default Exchange Coupling, keep Default Exchange Coupling connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Worker Effect Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A worker should acknowledge before starting slow work to prevent duplicates.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Worker Effect Evidence condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Worker Effect Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Worker Effect Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab messaging team handles case 1265 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Worker Effect Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Worker Effect Evidence, keep Worker Effect Evidence connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Worker Effect Evidence, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Worker Effect Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Worker Effect Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A lab messaging team handles case 1265 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Worker Effect Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A worker should acknowledge before starting slow work to prevent duplicates.
